howItWorks.intro: This umbrella insurance calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Recommended = net worth rounded up to nearest $1M (minimum $1M)
howItWorks.outro: Insurance rates depend on many individual factors. Get personalized quotes from licensed providers.

faq.question: What is umbrella insurance?
faq.answer: Extra liability coverage that kicks in when your auto or home insurance limits are exhausted. A $1M umbrella policy costs about $150-300/year and provides crucial protection for your assets.

faq.question: Who needs umbrella insurance?
faq.answer: Anyone with assets to protect. If your net worth exceeds your auto/home liability limits, you are exposed. Also important if you have a pool, trampoline, dog, teen driver, or rental property.

faq.question: How much does it cost?
faq.answer: First $1M of coverage costs about $150-300/year. Each additional $1M costs about $75-100/year. It is remarkably affordable for the protection provided.

faq.question: What does it cover?
faq.answer: Bodily injury, property damage, libel, slander, and certain lawsuits. It does NOT cover your own injuries, your own property, criminal acts, or business liability.
